text: Modern websites handle sensitive user data, making security a top priority in web development. HTTPS encrypts information sent between browsers and servers, preventing hackers from intercepting passwords or credit card details. This means both authentication processes and regular browsing stay protected. Developers must also validate all user input, such as form fields or search boxes, to block malicious code like SQL injections or cross-site scripting attacks. Common vulnerabilities include weak password storage and outdated software. By using HTTPS, implementing strong authentication measures, and carefully checking user input, developers can significantly reduce these risks and create safer online experiences for everyone.
